steps.title: Inspect Command step V_c(t)
steps.concept: External command source drives node n_cmd through a Simscape controlled voltage source.
steps.student_question: What should happen to V_m immediately after the command voltage V_c(t) changes?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [VC_STEP] and nodes [n_cmd, n_ref]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Finite-gain amplifier saturation and current limit
steps.concept: Amplifier error is sensed physically, multiplied by A_ol, rail-limited, driven by a controlled voltage source, and passed through a current limiter.
steps.student_question: How would finite gain or rail saturation prevent the clamp from forcing V_m exactly to V_c?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [AMP1] and nodes [n_cmd, n_feedback, n_amp_out, n_ref]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Electrode series resistance R_e
steps.concept: Clamp current flows from the amplifier output through the limiter, current sensor, and R_e into the membrane node.
steps.student_question: When clamp current increases, where does the voltage drop across R_e appear?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [RELECT, ICLAMP_SENSOR] and nodes [n_amp_out, n_mem_drive, n_vm]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Membrane C_m, R_m, and leak current
steps.concept: The membrane node is a Simscape electrical node with C_m, R_m, leak current, and optional nonlinear current to reference.
steps.student_question: Which part of the membrane model stores charge, and which part leaks current at steady state?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [MEMBRANE, INL_MEM] and nodes [n_vm, n_ref]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Vm and clamp-current probes
steps.concept: Voltage and current sensors feed PS-Simulink converters and To Workspace logs for probe responses.
steps.student_question: Which measured signal tells us whether the clamp succeeded, and which tells us how hard the amplifier worked?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [VM_SENSOR, ICLAMP_SENSOR] and nodes [n_vm, n_amp_out, n_mem_drive]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect ADC sampling and quantization
steps.concept: Measured V_m is sampled at T_s and quantized with V_adc_ref/(2^N_bits - 1).
steps.student_question: What information is lost when V_m is sampled and quantized into ADC codes?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [ADC1] and nodes [s_vm, s_adc_code]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect Digital comparator or finite-state logic
steps.concept: Sampled command and ADC code are differenced, converted to a symbolic control update K_ctrl, saturated, and logged with state and flag outputs.
steps.student_question: What state should the digital controller enter when the ADC code remains near the target for several samples?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [DIGCTRL] and nodes [s_adc_code, s_state, s_saturation_flags, s_control_update]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.

steps.title: Inspect DAC or command update feedback path
steps.concept: The saturated digital update is held, converted to a physical signal, and drives a controlled voltage source at n_feedback.
steps.student_question: How does a delayed DAC update change the closed-loop settling behavior?
steps.expected_reasoning: Use the selected analysis reasoning around components [DAC1, AMP1, DIGCTRL] and nodes [s_control_update, n_feedback, n_cmd]. Explain the physical quantity before computing it.
steps.reveal_hint: Name the component, node, or physical quantity first; then relate it to KCL/KVL, impedance, or energy storage.
steps.common_mistake: Jumping to a numeric answer before checking polarity, units, or the measured node.
